videos.title: 22.07.26 - RIFT Education Day
videos.summary_source_text:
22.07.26 - RIFT Education Day RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

RIFT is built around a simple vision: making modern trading technology more accessible while helping users participate in financial markets through a combination of automation, education, and innovative infrastructure. videos.title: Texas Rangers Need Wins To Win Over President Of Baseball Ops Chris Young | DLLS Rangers Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Texas Rangers Need Wins To Win Over President Of Baseball Ops Chris Young | DLLS Rangers Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

An ALLCITY Network Production

SUBSCRIBE: https://www.youtube.com/@DLLS_Sports

The Texas Rangers are back in first place as they continue their march toward the trade deadline. President of baseball operations Chris Young told writer on Monday that he needs to see winning baseball, wins stacked on wins, to be convinced that the Rangers are worth the investment at the Aug. 3 deadline. Young still believes the Rangers’ best baseball is ahead of them, and it hinges on health. Corey Seager needs to return, and he might be getting closer. The Rangers, though, are willing to set a date for his return. The returns of starters Jordan Montgomery, Cody Bradford and Jack Leiter would also help, both in the rotation and bullpen.

videos.title: Hindi - 22.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 22.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

videos.title: Why the Robertson Contract is Really an 8 Year Deal | Are Teams Less Interested in Naming a Captain?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why the Robertson Contract is Really an 8 Year Deal | Are Teams Less Interested in Naming a Captain? Dallas Stars lock up Jason Robertson with a 1-year, $12 million deal—does this short-term agreement foreshadow a decade of Avalanche headaches? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the real motive behind this “bridge” contract, the cap implications, and why all signs point to a mega-extension as soon as January 1st. With Dallas poised to retain their offensive powerhouse and $41 million in cap space next season, how will this affect Central Division rivals like the Colorado Avalanche?

The hosts spotlight how Robertson’s decision compares to former Avalanche star Mikko Rantanen’s exit and explore whether this contract model could set a new NHL trend as the salary cap climbs. Plus, they tackle the shifting significance of captaincy in the NHL, teams going leaderless, and what this means for the Colorado Avalanche’s leadership structure. Can the Avalanche stay competitive with Robertson locked in Dallas for years to come?

videos.title: Ryan Fox wins The Open, 2026 Major Season Reactions | The Drop
videos.summary_source_text:
Ryan Fox wins The Open, 2026 Major Season Reactions | The Drop The Drop wraps up the 2026 major season with a reaction to Ryan Fox’s win at The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. We’ll examine the importance of achieving this career highlight and how impressive it was to watch him get it done. After giving Fox his flowers, the show dives into some of the other key storylines from England – including an assessment of Scottie Scheffler’s major season, the most surprising result of The Open and a discussion on the rules of golf and how penalties are conveyed to players during the course of a tournament. Later in the show, Oklahoma State’s Preston Stout – who was the individual medalist at the 2026 NCAA Tournament – joins us to be awarded the top spot on the preseason PGA TOUR University Ranking sponsored by Huntington Bank. The rising senior talks about his accomplishments in college, the honor of this top spot and how he plans on playing this week when he tees it up with the pros at the 3M Open. And with 3 weeks left until the PGA TOUR’s regular season wraps up, we’ll discuss five players who are in and around the top-70 of the FedExCup Standings and break down their chances of making it to the postseason.

00:35 Ryan Fox wins The Open

04:58 Sam Burns Majors Recap

videos.title: This Is Why You Struggle To Spray Snow
videos.summary_source_text:
This Is Why You Struggle To Spray Snow The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: BREAKING: Jim Nill Explains Jason Robertson's 1-Year Stars Contract
videos.summary_source_text:
BREAKING: Jim Nill Explains Jason Robertson's 1-Year Stars Contract Dallas Stars General Manager Jim Nill meets with the media after the Stars reached a 1-year contract agreement with Jason Robertson, avoiding salary arbitration.

Why did the two sides agree to a one-year contract instead of a long-term extension? What does this deal mean for Robertson's future in Dallas? And what does Jim Nill have to say about the negotiations and the organization's outlook moving forward?

Watch the full Jim Nill press conference as he discusses Jason Robertson's new contract, the arbitration process, the Stars' salary cap situation, and what comes next for one of the NHL's top scorers.

videos.title: How to Ease into Fasting
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Ease into Fasting The goal of fasting isn't to make yourself miserable. The goal is to gradually change your metabolism. As your body adapts, it develops the ability to use both glucose and ketones, improving metabolic flexibility and supporting healthier mitochondrial function.

This is a clip from my conversation on The @JesseChappus Show. You can find the conversation on their channel or in the collaborations on my channel.

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Aristotle Education and has been practicing medicine in Central Florida for over 35 years. Throughout his distinguished career, he has performed thousands of interventional procedures in both hospital and outpatient settings. After Signing A 1-Year Extension, What’s Next For Jason Robertson & The Stars? | DLLS Stars Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

The Dallas Stars and restricted free agent forward Jason Robertson agreed to a one-year, $12 million contract on Tuesday evening, avoiding their arbitration hearing that was scheduled for Saturday, July 25th in Toronto. This is a short-term bridge contract that locks up Robertson for the 2026-27 season, but he can become an unrestricted free agent on July 1, 2027. On today’s show, we will talk about this deal and what it means not only for this season, but also for Robertson’s future with the Stars.

videos.title: Jason Robertson's $12M Deal + Jake Allen Joins | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Jason Robertson's $12M Deal + Jake Allen Joins | Daily Faceoff Live Jason Robertson and the Dallas Stars avoid arbitration with a one-year, $12 million contract, but what does the deal mean for Robertson's long-term future and Dallas' Stanley Cup window?

Tyler Yaremchuk and Carter Hutton break down why Robertson chose a short-term deal, whether he'll remain in Dallas beyond next season, and how the Stars could approach his next contract.

The guys also discuss the massive arbitration gap between the Columbus Blue Jackets and goaltender Jet Greaves, Connor Bedard's new contract with the Chicago Blackhawks, and the latest offseason storylines surrounding Dylan Larkin, Morgan Rielly, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes and Nikita Kucherov.

Later, New Jersey Devils goaltender Jake Allen joins the show to discuss preparing for a larger workload next season, the evolution of NHL goaltending, Canada's pipeline of elite goalies, three-goalie systems becoming more common around the league, his experience working Hockey Night in Canada, and the players who surprised him most in practice throughout his NHL career.

If you're looking for the latest NHL news, contract analysis, goalie insight, and offseason discussion, this episode has you covered.

videos.title: How Loose Should My Muscles Be For Hockey?
videos.summary_source_text:
How Loose Should My Muscles Be For Hockey? Looseness is a trained skill, and it's the reason the smartest players on the ice tend to also be the smoothest.

When you're tense, your muscles are all working against each other, where the movers are firing while the muscles that should be resting fire right alongside them, and that wasted effort is one of the clearest things separating a beginner from an elite mover. 

The best athletes in any profession have trained that unnecessary tension out, which is why their movement looks effortless even while they're going faster than everyone around them.

This is where it gets interesting, because your brain only has so much bandwidth in any given moment. When your motor system is working overtime just to keep you upright and moving, it eats up the resources you'd otherwise spend reading the ice. Relaxed, efficient movement quiets that demand, and the attention and working memory it frees up is the exact thing we call hockey sense: seeing the play develop, holding your options in your head.

It also explains why some players shrink in big games. Under pressure you tense up, a skill you've drilled a thousand times suddenly needs conscious control again, your focus turns inward onto your own feet, and you stop seeing the ice. Same player, less looseness, and the game speeds up around them.

The catch is you can't just tell yourself to relax in the middle of a shift. Looseness has to be built into the mechanics themselves so it holds up when the speed and the pressure climb, and that's the whole reason we teach efficient movement first in the Downhill Skating System, so your brain is free to read the game instead of managing your feet.

videos.title: Episode 52: The Dog Days | Tri-State Hockey Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Episode 52: The Dog Days | Tri-State Hockey Podcast Jonny Lazarus, Arthur Staple and Mike Rupp are back together for Episode 52 of the Tri-State Hockey Podcast to assess where the New Jersey Devils, New York Rangers and New York Islanders stand following their offseason moves.

The guys begin with the Devils and debate whether New Jersey has the strongest roster of the three teams. They break down where Anthony Mantha could fit alongside Jack Hughes and Jesper Bratt, the improved versatility of the forward group, concerns surrounding the defence and whether Jake Allen, Nico Daws and David Rittich can provide enough stability in goal.

The conversation then shifts to the Rangers following an active offseason. Arthur explains why he liked Chris Drury’s moves, while the group discusses the Vincent Trocheck trade, Pavel Dorofeyev, the Rangers’ lack of depth down the middle and the pressure facing J.T. Miller, Alexis Lafrenière, Gabe Perreault and head coach Mike Sullivan.

Finally, the guys examine the Islanders’ quiet summer, Anthony Duclair’s future, whether veteran players could block opportunities for younger talent and what Peter DeBoer can get out of the current roster. They also discuss the Islanders’ struggling power play, Matthew Schaefer’s role and whether the team did enough to improve. The show closes with a Tri-State trivia challenge featuring every Rangers, Islanders and Devils player to record a 30-goal season since 2003-04.

TIMESTAMPS

2:20 Ranking the best forwards across the three teams

videos.summary_source_text:
I haven't eaten sugar for a year. Sugar-free protein bars in 5 minutes. I haven't eaten sugar for a year. Sugar-free protein bars in 5 minutes.

1 cup (100 g) almond flour.
1 cup (250 g) peanut butter.
5 Medjool dates.
If the mixture is too soft, simply add a little more almond flour until you reach the desired consistency.
Honestly, the best part is how simple this is — you just mix everything together, press it into the pan, and pour that melted chocolate right over the top.
No baking, no fuss — it all comes together in one bowl, and the chocolate layer does all the hard work of making it look fancy.
3.5 oz (100 g) melted dark chocolate mixed with 3 tbsp milk.
Place in the fridge for 1 hr.
It's basically a protein-packed dessert — rich, chocolatey, and just the thing when you want something sweet without the guilt.

videos.summary_source_text:
What Happens When You Eat a Banana Before Bed?  The Best Time to Eat It | Dr. Mandell Could eating a banana before bed be one of the simplest healthy habits you can add to your evening routine? In this video, I explain what really happens in your body when you eat a banana at night, including how its natural potassium, magnesium, vitamin B6, fiber, and carbohydrates may support muscle function, overnight recovery, digestive health, and overall wellness. I'll also discuss who may benefit most from eating a banana before bed, who may want to be more cautious, and the best way to enjoy it as part of a balanced diet. If you've ever wondered whether nighttime is actually the best time to eat a banana, this video will help you understand the science in a simple, practical way so you can make the best choice for your health.

videos.title: Stars Buy Time With A Robertson Extension...But What Comes Next? | GS Off Ice Off Szn
videos.summary_source_text:
Stars Buy Time With A Robertson Extension...But What Comes Next? | GS Off Ice Off Szn Welcome back to another special edition of Guerilla Sports Off Ice! 

Jesse Montano and Mark Robinson are back for more league-wide NHL talk, hitting all the hottest off-season hockey topics that you can think of.

Jason Robertson and the Dallas Stars have reached an agreement and avoided arbitration. Robo signed a 1 year, 12 million dollar contract to stay in Dallas for the forseeable future. Right now, it appears Dallas has spent 12 million dollars on a little bit of extra time. Whether it be a trade or another extension, either outcome is still imminent. The Stars have major cap issues that need to be solved now that they can table getting Robertson locked up long term. What is the tell behind this move, and what comes next for Dallas?

Jay Zawaski also joins the show from the CHGO studios to talk about Connor Bedard's recent extension and other news surrounding the Chicago Blackhawks.

🏒Connor Bedard Extension
🏒The Yzerplan has come to a screeching hault

videos.title: GPT 6 The 4 TRILLION Parameter Reset Nobody Expected
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT 6 The 4 TRILLION Parameter Reset Nobody Expected Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
OpenAI is now seven releases deep into the GPT-5 generation, yet there is still no official GPT-6 announcement, model card, or confirmed release date.

In this video, we examine what may be happening behind the scenes at OpenAI—from the reported government review connected to GPT-5.6 and the alleged scrapping of the original GPT-6 “Spud” training run to the scheduled retirement of GPT-5.4.

The bigger question is not whether GPT-5.6 is capable. It is whether OpenAI is losing momentum, certainty, and release-cadence credibility while Anthropic, xAI, and Google continue advancing their own frontier AI models.

We break down:

• Why GPT-5.6 reportedly faced an external government review
• Why seven GPT-5 releases have arrived without GPT-6
• What the reported “Scrapped Spud” reset could mean
• Why GPT-5.4’s retirement matters
• How OpenAI compares with Claude, Grok, and Gemini
• What developers building on the OpenAI API should do now
• Why frontier AI models are becoming harder to release on schedule
• What we still do—and do not—know about GPT-6

Is OpenAI genuinely falling behind, or is the entire AI industry discovering that the next generational leap requires more time, money, scrutiny, and uncertainty?

Share your GPT-6 release-date prediction in the comments.

Subscribe for evidence-based coverage of OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Google DeepMind, GPT-5, GPT-6, Claude, Grok, Gemini, AGI, and the rapidly changing artificial-intelligence race.

Chapters

00:00 OpenAI Has a GPT-6 Problem
01:30 The Government Review Nobody Fully Explained
02:40 Seven Releases Deep, Still No GPT-6
03:53 What “Scrapped Spud” Actually Tells You
05:02 GPT-5.4’s Retirement Is the Real Signal
05:50 The AGI Stakes
07:02 What This Means If You’re Building on GPT Today
08:17 The Broader Pattern Worth Watching
09:33 The Limitations Nobody’s Putting in the Thumbnail
10:31 The Verdict

videos.title: Robertson's Deal, Hellebuyck's Future, & Columbus Cap Crunch | DFO Rundown Insider Edition
videos.summary_source_text:
Robertson's Deal, Hellebuyck's Future, & Columbus Cap Crunch | DFO Rundown Insider Edition Jason Robertson finally has a contract, but does that mean everything is back to normal in Dallas? Irfaan Gaffar and David Pagnotta break down the Stars forward's one-year deal, why a long-term extension doesn't appear imminent, and why there may still be lingering tension between Robertson and the organization. They also dive into the Columbus Blue Jackets' contract situation, what's next for the Detroit Red Wings organization, and how offer sheets are continuing to reshape the NHL landscape.

The guys also discuss the latest surrounding Connor Hellebuyck and the Winnipeg Jets, contract negotiations involving Arber Xhekaj, Will Smith, and Macklin Celebrini, plus where things stand with Cutter Gauthier and the Anaheim Ducks. It's another packed summer edition of DFO Insider Edition with all the latest news, rumours, and contract updates from around the NHL.

01:12 - Robertson signs

Irfaan Gaffar and David Pagnotta break down the Stars forward's one-year deal, why a long-term extension doesn't appear imminent, and why there may still be lingering tension between Robertson and the organization. The guys also discuss the latest surrounding Connor Hellebuyck and the Winnipeg Jets, contract negotiations involving Arber Xhekaj, Will Smith, and Macklin Celebrini, plus where things stand with Cutter Gauthier and the Anaheim Ducks. The #1 Healthiest Bread in the World Find the full recipe here: ▶️https://youtu.be/vNdpBvSCpyU

The healthiest bread isn’t the whole wheat loaf sitting on your grocery store shelf. This simple 5-ingredient bread is a low-carb alternative that avoids the blood sugar spikes associated with both whole wheat and white bread.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/44EQg54

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Why make your own bread?
• Only 5 simple ingredients.
• A low-carb alternative to traditional bread.
• Whole wheat bread and white bread have a similar glycemic index, meaning they can have a comparable effect on blood sugar.
• Designed for those looking to avoid traditional breads while still enjoying homemade bread.

If you’re looking for a healthier bread recipe that’s easy to make and supports a low-carb lifestyle, try this homemade keto-friendly version.

videos.title: Anthropic, ChatGPT & Gemini AI Safety Tests Exposed — What Really Happened
videos.summary_source_text:
Anthropic, ChatGPT & Gemini AI Safety Tests Exposed — What Really Happened Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Anthropic’s Claude, OpenAI’s ChatGPT and GPT models, Google Gemini, Grok and DeepSeek were placed under the spotlight in a series of major AI safety and security tests—but the results were far more complicated than the headlines suggest.

In this AI news roundup, we examine claims that frontier AI models attempted to cheat during cybersecurity evaluations, why Claude reportedly refused to threaten another AI in a separate coercion benchmark, and what these results actually tell us about AI alignment and model safety.

We also break down Anthropic’s reported $1.5 billion copyright settlement, security disclosures involving AI coding agents such as OpenAI Codex, Cursor and Google Gemini CLI, and Google’s latest Gemini development announcement.

This video covers:

• Anthropic Claude and OpenAI GPT safety evaluations
• ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok and DeepSeek benchmark behavior
• Claude Sonnet and Claude Opus coercion-test results
• Anthropic’s reported $1.5B AI copyright settlement
• OpenAI Codex, Cursor and Gemini CLI sandbox vulnerabilities
• Google Gemini’s latest model-development update
• The difference between verified AI news and exaggerated headlines

The biggest lesson is not simply which AI model scored highest. It is whether systems from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google and other major AI companies can reliably follow rules when they are placed under pressure.

Watch until the end for the full verdict, and comment with the story you think deserves a separate deep dive.

Subscribe for evidence-based coverage of ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, artificial intelligence, AI safety, cybersecurity and the latest technology news.

06:16 The Sandbox Was Never Locked

10:04 The Verdict

videos.title: Gemini 3.6 Flash Is Here — Google’s 3 New Models vs Claude and OpenAI
videos.summary_source_text:
Gemini 3.6 Flash Is Here — Google’s 3 New Models vs Claude and OpenAI Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Google just launched three new Gemini AI models in a single afternoon: Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and the restricted-access Flash Cyber.

But while Gemini 3.6 Flash is designed to become Google’s new fast, affordable AI workhorse—and Flash-Lite targets high-volume, low-cost applications—the most interesting model may be the one almost nobody is allowed to use.

In this video, we break down Google’s three-model AI launch and separate confirmed facts from launch-day hype. We examine the claimed coding and agentic improvements in Gemini 3.6 Flash, explain who Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite is really designed for, and investigate why Flash Cyber is restricted to sensitive government and cybersecurity work.

We also look at the bigger AI race involving Google, Claude, and OpenAI—and why major AI labs are increasingly creating locked-down cyber models that cannot be independently tested or benchmarked.

The launch generated serious attention, including coverage reportedly reaching more than 1,500 views per hour and search estimates of approximately 17,700 monthly US searches for Gemini 3.6 Flash. But popularity is not proof of performance.

Watch before switching models or believing the biggest Gemini headlines.

In This Video
• Gemini 3.6 Flash coding and agentic performance
• Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite pricing and use cases
• Google Flash Cyber restricted access
• Google Gemini vs Claude and OpenAI
• Independent AI benchmark concerns
• Artificial Analysis verification
• AI cybersecurity and government models
• Which Gemini model developers should use

videos.title: What is the Purpose of Stress?
videos.summary_source_text:
What is the Purpose of Stress? What Is the Purpose of Stress?

Most of us think of stress as the enemy. But what if stress is actually your body's way of preparing you for action?

In this clip, neuroscientist @lfeldmanbarrett explains that stress isn't a design flaw. It's an evolutionary adaptation. Your brain is preparing your body for a big metabolic demand. Thousands of years ago that might have meant hunting, gathering, or escaping danger. Today it might mean a difficult meeting, parenting, or hours of focused knowledge work.

The chemistry hasn't changed. Our environment has.

One practical takeaway: when you feel stressed, move. Go for a walk. Practice yoga. Lift weights. Stretch. Give your body the physical outlet it's preparing for, instead of getting stuck in rumination and overthinking.

Thanks to @lfeldmanbarrett for helping us better understand why our brains and bodies respond to stress the way they do.

Question: What helps you break the cycle of stress and overwhelm? What Is the Purpose of Stress?

videos.title: Chip Like a Pro | Part 2 | Padraig Harrington
videos.summary_source_text:
Chip Like a Pro | Part 2 | Padraig Harrington As part of my YouTube series  “Ten lessons every golfer should know”, the sixth Paddy’s Golf Tip is on chipping, which is in two parts. 
 
In this second and final instalment,  I provide some insights on chipping, including judging where to land the ball on the green having assessed the risk attached and why practising putting from off the greens can be a useful option.  In addition, I highlight why using one specific club for all chips, until it is clearly not the right club, can be effective.

I also offer a tip for practising on the course using a number of balls, which can provide useful feedback and ultimately help get the ball "up and down"!

#PadraigHarrington #golfing #golftips #paddysgolftips #golf #feedback #chipping 

SUBSCRIBE FOR MORE CONTENT: https://bit.ly/4gKoVme

videos.title: Racing the World’s BIGGEST Plane in the FASTEST Porsche Ever!
videos.summary_source_text:
Racing the World’s BIGGEST Plane in the FASTEST Porsche Ever! We’re drag racing the most powerful Porsche production car, the Cayenne Turbo Electric, against the Stratolaunch Roc - the world’s largest operating aircraft by wingspan! 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-2207

It’s time for the craziest Carwow drag race ever! 

Mat’s teamed up with Porsche over in America, and he’s about to race the most powerful Porsche production car (the Cayenne Turbo Electric) against one of the largest planes in the world - the Stratolaunch Roc! 

There’ll be a series of two races - one against the plane as it takes off, and another as it lands! This race is fraught with danger, as this plane is absolutely massive, and any wrong move could be disastrous! So let’s check out the two contenders. 

The Stratolaunch Roc has the largest wingspan of any plane in the world, at 117 metres - longer than an American football field! It’s powered by six engines from a Boeing 747, weighs over 220 tonnes, and has a top speed of 460 knots (530mph)! 

As for the Cayenne Turbo Electric, it’s powered by dual electric motors, and these combine to produce 1,139hp and 1,500Nm. As for the weight, it tips the scales at 2,645kg. 

So how’s this one going to play out? Stick with Mat to see for yourself! We’re drag racing the most powerful Porsche production car, the Cayenne Turbo Electric, against the Stratolaunch Roc - the world’s largest operating aircraft by wingspan!

The #1 Sign You're Deficient in Potassium (It's NOT Muscle Cramps) The number one sign you need more potassium may not be muscle cramps, fatigue, or weakness; it’s high blood pressure.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4fTTpot

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Why potassium is so important:
• Helps support healthy blood pressure
• Helps relax the arteries
• Supports energy production
• Supports heart health
• Supports kidney function
• Helps regulate excess fluid in the body

The body needs about 4700 mg of potassium per day, yet many people struggle to get that much from food. Avocados and leafy green salads are two of the richest sources.

#potassium #highbloodpressure #bloodpressure #hypertension #hearthealth #kidneyhealth #electrolytes #nutrition #healthtips #wellness

videos.title: Why Moving Slowly Works Better Than Trying Hard
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Moving Slowly Works Better Than Trying Hard Why can moving slowly create deeper change than trying harder?
Because real learning happens when the body has time to sense what it is doing.
Many people try to improve movement through more effort, more repetition, and more correction. But if the body is already bracing or protecting, trying harder may only repeat the same pattern.
In this video, I explain why slow movement helps the nervous system recognize old patterns and discover new possibilities. You’ll also experience a short somatic exploration with gentle head turning.
Try my free guided somatic movement session here: https://taro-iwamoto.thinkific.com/courses/masterclass

Read my new book:
Your Body Is Not Broken: A Gentle Somatic Path to Moving with More Ease, Trust, and Freedom
A short Kindle book for anyone tired of trying to fix their body and wanting a gentler way to begin again.
https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0GX363HMX

If you feel ready for deeper, more structured support, you can learn more about my course here:
https://taroiwamoto.com/thecourse

videos.summary_source_text:
Nick Ferguson Breaks Down Training Camp | Broncos 2026 Season Predictions, Bo Nix | After the Horn The Denver Broncos are just days away from training camp, and there's plenty to discuss.

This week on After the Horn, Tom Green and Woody Paige are joined by former Broncos safety Nick Ferguson for an in-depth conversation about the biggest storylines surrounding the 2026 Denver Broncos. From realistic expectations for Bo Nix's second season to Jonathan Cooper's uncertain future, Davis Webb taking over play-calling duties, the Broncos' running game, and why Denver's defense could once again carry the team, the crew breaks down everything fans need to know before camp opens.

Plus, Nick shares incredible behind-the-scenes stories from his playing career, including the famous Champ Bailey interception against the Patriots, defending elite NFL tight ends, and what it takes to play safety in today's NFL.

The show also dives into the FIFA World Cup Final, Colorado football, Georgia Tech vs. Colorado, and much more.

videos.title: This New App Gave Me an AI Team of Employees
videos.summary_source_text:
This New App Gave Me an AI Team of Employees Join my community to access this Buzz 👉 https://mrc.fm/cmc
🐝 The Buzz walkthrough reposted by Jack Dorsey.

Yesterday Jack Dorsey launched Buzz (buzz.xyz) a new app from Block that lets AI agents join your team like real colleagues, aimed squarely at Slack and GitHub. Almost nobody has got it running yet, so in this video I self host Buzz on my own VPS, where I hold the keys and nobody else can read my messages, and build out a full AI team inside it. I meet the starter agents, wire them up to Claude Code and Codex, create my own agents from scratch, and then build the headline feature everyone wanted to see... a chief of staff agent that delegates to all my other agents so I only ever talk to one. I also dig into the stuff no one else has covered, like sharing my own compute so my community can run local models peer to peer, and I give you my honest day one verdict on where Buzz beats Slack and where it still falls short.

Try Buzz 🐝 https://buzz.xyz

videos.title: Losing Muscle After Menopause? Protein Isn’t Enough (Do THIS 2x a Week)
videos.summary_source_text:
Losing Muscle After Menopause? Protein Isn’t Enough (Do THIS 2x a Week) Postmenopausal muscle loss is a real problem many women deal with, but it doesn’t have to be. Find out how to build muscle after menopause to help you stay healthy, strong, and youthful.

0:00 Menopause muscle loss
1:55 Estrogen and muscle loss
2:50 How to build muscle after menopause 
5:00 Sarcopenia 
6:19 Hormone therapy and postmenopausal muscle loss
7:24 Protein for menopause 
7:45 Best protein sources for muscle growth
9:05 Diet tips for postmenopausal muscle loss
11:02 Muscle-building tips

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4pxwX7a

Explore the comprehensive guide to building and preserving muscle after menopause: https://drbrg.co/45hg4o2

Keep watching to learn more about maintaining strength and thriving after menopause:
▶️ https://youtu.be/XPDzypsisI4
▶️ https://youtu.be/vWrzH4IBs-Q
▶️ https://youtu.be/1nAfTsObuwY

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

There’s a three-year window in every woman’s life when she can lose more muscle than during the previous 20 years combined. Even with plenty of protein and regular exercise, many women still experience postmenopausal muscle loss.

Leucine is an essential amino acid that signals your muscles to grow. As you age, your protein and leucine requirements increase, making both especially important for maintaining muscle after menopause.

After menopause, estrogen levels drop significantly, contributing to postmenopausal muscle loss. Fortunately, exercise can help rebuild muscle. Research has shown that weight training can significantly improve muscle loss after menopause while also supporting bone health.

Age-related muscle loss is known as sarcopenia. It affects not only muscle tissue but also the nervous system and connective tissue, making it more difficult to rebuild muscle over time. This is why it’s important to begin preserving and rebuilding muscle as early as possible.

DATA:

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

There’s a three-year window in every woman’s life when she can lose more muscle than during the previous 20 years combined. Even with plenty of protein and regular exercise, many women still experience postmenopausal muscle loss.

Leucine is an essential amino acid that signals your muscles to grow. As you age, your protein and leucine requirements increase, making both especially important for maintaining muscle after menopause.

After menopause, estrogen levels drop significantly, contributing to postmenopausal muscle loss. Fortunately, exercise can help rebuild muscle. Research has shown that weight training can significantly improve muscle loss after menopause while also supporting bone health.

Age-related muscle loss is known as sarcopenia. It affects not only muscle tissue but also the nervous system and connective tissue, making it more difficult to rebuild muscle over time. This is why it’s important to begin preserving and rebuilding muscle as early as possible.

BRAND NEW Odyssey Turtle putter 😍🐢 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

buying your first proper set,

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

• Full 18-hole course vlogs

• Matches against tour players and creators

• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

videos.title: THE SHOW GOES ON! Carolina Hurricanes Announce the Launch of Their Own Sports Network
videos.summary_source_text:
THE SHOW GOES ON! Carolina Hurricanes Announce the Launch of Their Own Sports Network Good news, everyone! We have an idea of which sports network the Carolina Hurricanes will be on next season! It'll be their own! What does this mean for the team and the fans? On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes,  Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the announcement of the Hurricanes launching their own sports network. Since the closing of the regional broadcasts, i.e., FanDuel, Bally, etc., what does it mean to focus on a more local, team-specific platform? Also, we know who will call games for the Hurricanes in 2026-27. Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on why this new era of Hurricanes broadcasting will be better for the team and fans overall. Since the team will own and operate it all, it could mean better production and just having it be done in-house, while working in collaboration with the NHL. Finally, Zach shares more Canes news, especially the annual Canes 5K, which is held before the preseason. What other news is surrounding the Hurricanes as we are another day closer to the 2026-27 season? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: 21.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
21.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

RIFT is built around a simple vision: making modern trading technology more accessible while helping users participate in financial markets through a combination of automation, education, and innovative infrastructure. videos.title: Why Jim Mora & Co. must revive CSU’s rushing attack + celebrating Leon Fuller with Marty Lenz
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Jim Mora & Co. must revive CSU’s rushing attack + celebrating Leon Fuller with Marty Lenz Justin Michael talks a little bit about Leon Fuller’s passing and what his legacy means to Colorado State. Justin also talks about Cecil Sapp’s legendary run alongside Bradlee Van Pelt in the early 2000’s, as well as why he’s so adamant about CSU Football needing to more effectively run the football in the Jim Mora era. Finally, Justin is joined by longtime radio broadcaster and former CSU Football player Marty Lenz to discuss Fuller and the infamous 1986 season.

videos.title: AJ Jones Has The Most Iconic Trick Shot On Golf Social Media | Closed Course
videos.summary_source_text:
AJ Jones Has The Most Iconic Trick Shot On Golf Social Media | Closed Course Welcome back to another episode of Closed Course!

The guys are back with another great interview, this time catching up with golf trick-shot master AJ Jones. From long drive competitions to viral golf social media videos, AJ has built a phenomenal following in the golf community through his unique approach to growing a love for the game. A former Division I baseball player at Long Beach State University, AJ transitioned into golf content creation after his collegiate career. He credits his baseball background for the incredible trick shots that require elite hand-eye coordination. AJ's brand isn't just about golf, either. Much of his content is centered around charity work and giving back to the community.

From his iconic stinger trick shot to his time and accolades as a professional long driver, this interview with AJ is a must-listen for golf fans.

You can find AJ on Instagram and TikTok @ajjonesgolf, and you can visit his website at ajjonesgolf.com.

After wrapping things up with AJ, the guys get into some general golf talk, including the Korn Ferry Tour and how more celebrities are starting to get into the game, including Justin Gaethje.
Tune in to the Closed Course podcast every Tuesday at 6:00 a.m. for great golf conversations and unforgettable interviews with all sorts of unique guests.

Hosted by Blake Horstmann, Nick Mertz, Erik Bradley, and Liam Johnson

Powered by Pins and Aces

videos.title: “Unless there was a deadline, Pat’s gonna use all the leverage he can to grind this down.”
videos.summary_source_text:

videos.title: This Is Why You Struggle To Grip The Ice
videos.summary_source_text:
This Is Why You Struggle To Grip The Ice The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

The Other 31: Pittsburgh Penguins

We started our summer series with the Philadelphia Flyers and PHLY’s Charlie O’Connor last week (July 13th). So, we are going to stick with the Metropolitan Division and talk about the Pittsburgh Penguins. Last summer we thought the Penguins were going to be bad - a non-playoff team, but not bad enough to tank for the Gavin McKenna top draft pick. The Pens surprised a lot of people by being pretty decent all year and making the playoffs, before losing to the Philadelphia Flyers in the first round. So, what are the next steps for GM Kyle Dubas and particularly for head coach Dan Muse, who now has one year under his belt as an NHL bench boss?

Google has now reportedly missed three expected release windows for Gemini 3.5 Pro—and this story goes much deeper than another delayed AI model.

In this video, we break down Bloomberg's reporting, the reported departures of senior Gemini researchers to Anthropic, Google's internal quality benchmarks, prediction market odds, and what all of this means if you're choosing an AI model today.

Instead of repeating headlines, we examine the evidence, separate confirmed reporting from speculation, and explain why this delay matters for developers, businesses, and anyone following the AI race.

videos.title: Your Brain Needs These 5 Foods | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Your Brain Needs These 5 Foods | Dr. Mandell Did you know that what you eat every week can have a powerful impact on your brain and cardiovascular health? In this video, Dr. Alan Mandell explains five science-backed foods that can support healthy blood vessels, improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and help maintain healthy blood pressure and cholesterol levels—all important factors for lowering stroke risk as part of a healthy lifestyle. Learn why berries, leafy green vegetables, fatty fish, beans and lentils, and extra virgin olive oil deserve a regular place on your plate, along with a bonus food that offers additional heart-healthy benefits. You'll also discover the physiology behind how these foods nourish your arteries and brain, along with simple ways to include them in your weekly meals. Small, consistent changes can make a meaningful difference over time. Watch until the end to learn practical tips that can help you build a healthier lifestyle and support your long-term brain and heart health. As always, consult your healthcare provider before making significant dietary changes, especially if you have an existing medical condition or take prescription medications.

The Modern Bunker Technique Making Amateurs Look Like Tour Pros Struggling with bunker shots? In this video, I team up with an ex-European Tour professional to reveal the modern bunker technique that many of today's Tour players use to escape the sand with more height, spin, and consistency.

We'll break down the key fundamentals behind great greenside bunker shots, including setup, clubface, body movement, and the common mistakes that stop golfers from getting out of bunkers first time.

If you want to improve your bunker play, lower your scores, and gain confidence from the sand, this lesson is packed with simple bunker tips you can take straight to the golf course.

Whether you're a beginner or a low handicap golfer, these golf bunker tips and short game drills will help you become a much better player around the greens.

How Will THESE Dallas Mavericks Get Enough Minutes? | DLLS Mavs Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

The Dallas Mavericks have a lot of players. That’s a good thing! But that also means there are a lot of guys deserving of minutes, and there are only so many of those to go around. The DLLS Mavs crew takes a crack at building their own rotation ahead of Cooper Flagg’s second and the first of the Masai Ujiri era, live on YouTube!

videos.title: Bedard signs his extension & Greaves arbitration filing ft. Greg Wyshynski & Jack Campbell
videos.summary_source_text:
Bedard signs his extension & Greaves arbitration filing ft. Greg Wyshynski & Jack Campbell On today’s episode of The Sheet, Jeff Marek is joined by Greg Wyshynski of ESPN. The big news of the weekend, Connor Bedard signing a 5 year, $15M AAV extension with Chicago. Jeff and Greg discuss that deal, the intricacies hidden in it, and what it means for the Blackhawks 5 year outlook.

They also dive into this morning's news of Jet Greaves arbitration filings. The young netminder and the Columbus Blue Jackets remain millions of dollars apart. Will they figure out a deal in the next 48 hours, or will an arbitrator have to decide whether Greaves is paid like a young backup goalie or a legit number one? Wysh also shares his thoughts on the NHL's evolving regional sports network saga and the league's broadcast bootcamp that took place recently.

Finally, we welcome former Dallas Stars, LA Kings, Toronto Maple Leafs, and Edmonton Oilers goaltender Jack Campbell to the show. Jack recently announced his retirement from hockey. He opens up about his mental health struggles, how he persevered through them, and what his post-playing career will look like.

#TheSheet #NHL #NHLOffseason #NHLFreeAgency #Hockey #MapleLeafs #Oilers #Kings #Stars #ESPN #NHLBroadcast #Blackhawks #chicagoblackhawks #Bedard #BlueJackets #ColumbusBlueJackets #Greaves

But today the members of 5SOS have two choices: Tell the truth, or suffer the wrath of the Last Dab. Whoever eats the most wings, loses!

GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra, Claude Fable 5, xAI Safety Report & AI Cyberattack Explained Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
This week in AI was packed with massive headlines—but the real story isn't just about smarter models. It's about control, safety, infrastructure, and verification.

In this video we break down:
✅ GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra's claimed proof of the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture
✅ The Future of Life Institute AI Safety Index and why xAI received an F
✅ Anthropic's massive compute deal and what it means for the AI hardware race
✅ Claude Fable 5 expanding to Max and Team Premium users
✅ JADEPUFFER—the reported first autonomous AI-powered ransomware attack

Rather than repeating headlines, we dig into the original sources, explain what has actually been confirmed, and separate facts from speculation.

videos.title: "Inget slår min Koenigsegg Jesko, men jag behöver andra sportbilar!"
videos.summary_source_text:
"Inget slår min Koenigsegg Jesko, men jag behöver andra sportbilar!" Ronnie Gustafsson lägger inte upp något på sociala medier, men för två år sedan fick jag intervjua honom om hans Koenigsegg CCXR – och det är min mest sedda film med 300 000 visningar.
Nu har jag fått en till exklusiv intervju på den fantastiska bilutställningen The Aurora, där Ronnie visade upp tio av sina favoritbilar, som bjöd på en "Bilsymfoni!"
Inget överskuggar hans Jesko, som han redan har hunnit köra på bana med två gånger och ska lära sig göra 360-svängar med. En bil för runt 35 miljoner …
Men i samlingen finns bilar som är magiska på andra vis. 
Vi snackar körglädje, bilutveckling, varför Jeskon har fått en alldeles speciell grön färg, favoriter, bilkörning, bankörning, restomods, familjen Koenigsegg, elbilar och om den tredje Koenigsegg han beställt …
Jag har en stående inbjudan till hans garage, där hela samlingen finns! Så det kommer mera längre fram.
Stort tack!

Se till att prenumerera så du inte missar något!

Se filmerna utan reklam före alla andra: 
Bli gärna medlem och stöd kanalen, som blir reklamfri! 29 kr/månad
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCU-4UJDGqQWK5Iv4wXDlzGQ/join

⚡️ Köp eller sälj elbil via Carla: https://carla.se

videos.title: Steve Yzerman Out, Robertson Arbitration & Hellebuyck Watch | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Steve Yzerman Out, Robertson Arbitration & Hellebuyck Watch | DFO Rundown Steve Yzerman is out as General Manager of the Detroit Red Wings, creating one of the biggest NHL storylines of the summer. What led to the surprising front office change, and what does it mean for Dylan Larkin, Moritz Seider, Lucas Raymond, and the future of the Red Wings?

Jason Gregor and Mike Rupp break down Detroit's next steps, discuss Jason Robertson's looming arbitration hearing with the Dallas Stars, and explain why his next contract could have ripple effects across the NHL. The guys also dive into Leo Carlsson's massive offer sheet, how the rising salary cap is changing player negotiations, and why blockbuster contracts are only going to become more common.

Plus, the latest on Connor Hellebuyck and whether the Winnipeg Jets star could still be on the move before the start of the NHL season, the NHL's new centralized broadcast production plans, Jet Greaves' arbitration case, training camp changes with the new 84-game schedule, and another edition of Fill in the Blank.

(Filmed July 21st, 9:45 a.m. ET)

Losing Muscle as You Get Older? Are you getting weak with age? 

Muscle doesn't care how old you are. It only responds to one question: have you been using it?

Strength training can help you:
• Build and maintain lean muscle
• Support stronger bones and healthier joints
• Improve balance and reduce the risk of falls
• Boost metabolic health

You don't need to spend hours in the gym. You need a safe, sustainable program you can stick with. That's lifestyle fitness: training to improve your everyday life, not take it over.

videos.title: What's Inside The TaylorMade Tour Truck? (Exclusive Pro Access!)
videos.summary_source_text:
What's Inside The TaylorMade Tour Truck? (Exclusive Pro Access!) Guy takes you inside the TaylorMade Tour Truck for an exclusive look at where the world’s best players have their clubs built, adjusted and perfected. From custom equipment to some very famous player drawers, this is a proper behind-the-scenes tour.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno replaces the low and slow golf swing takeaway with one simple mantra — wide and up — and two golf drills to make it stick.

If you've ever been told to keep the club low and slow in your golf swing takeaway, this video will make you rethink everything. Eric Cogorno shows exactly why low and slow sends the club too far inside and under plane — and replaces it with two simple mantras any golfer can use: wide and up with the arms and club, centered and turned with the body. Watch how Adam Scott and Nelly Korda start their golf backswing with the club head above the hip while the hands stay below — and how the lead hand and trail hand drills give you the same feeling in minutes. These golf backswing tips apply to any golfer regardless of handicap or ability level. If you leave this video without trying the lead hand only drill, you're leaving a better golf swing takeaway on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• Why low and slow in the golf swing takeaway sends the club too far inside and under plane
• The wide and up mantra: butt of the club outside the trail thigh, club head above the hip
• How Adam Scott and Nelly Korda execute a wide and up golf swing takeaway
• The centered and turned body mantra that prevents the upper body from drifting away from the target
• The lead hand only drill for training width and centered turn in the golf backswing
• The trail hand only drill for exaggerating width and feeling the correct takeaway position
• How to use the Swing Coach app to check swing plane and spine position during the takeaway
• How getting the golf swing takeaway right makes everything in the downswing easier

The #1 Best Way to Lower Blood Sugar Naturally The number one thing to lower blood sugar isn’t metformin, chromium, or cinnamon. Those approaches may help manage blood sugar, but they don’t address the underlying issue: insulin resistance.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4oERYN7

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

A low-carb diet targets insulin resistance directly rather than simply managing the symptom. Research has shown that low-carb diets can improve insulin resistance and, in many cases, help reverse type 2 diabetes within months.

videos.title: Who makes the STRONGEST engines? Merc v Toyota v Land Rover
videos.summary_source_text:
Who makes the STRONGEST engines? Merc v Toyota v Land Rover We test the strength and reliability of an old Toyota Land Cruiser, Land Rover Discovery and Mercedes ML by running the cars at full throttle for as long as possible. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-21-07

Who builds the strongest engines - Toyota, Land Rover, or Mercedes? 

Well, to find out, we’re pushing our £2,000 Toyota Land Cruiser, Land Rover Discovery and Mercedes ML to the limit to see which lasts the longest. 

They’re no longer road-worthy, but which can last the longest when pushed to full throttle?  First off, let’s take a closer look at the contenders: 

Starting with the Discovery 3, it’s powered by a 2.7-litre V6 that produces 190hp and 440Nm. It’s equipped with a 6-speed manual and weighs 2,419kg. It was first released in 2005, we bought it for £1,500, and it currently has 256,000 miles on the clock. 

Up next is the Mercedes ML. There’s a 2.7-litre inline-5 turbo diesel under the bonnet, and this produces 163hp and 400Nm. It’s equipped with a 5-speed automatic gearbox, weighs around 2,100kg, and was released in 2005. We bought it for £1,800, and it has 140,000 miles on the clock. 

Finally, we come to the Land Cruiser. It’s powered by a 4.7-litre V8, and this puts down 235hp and 434Nm. It rolls up with a 4-speed automatic gearbox, and it weighs 2,260kg. It was originally released in 2002, and although it was originally bought for £5,000, it’s only worth around £2,000 now. To date, it’s run around 282,000 miles.

So which will last the longest - Land Rover, Toyota or Mercedes? Drop your guess in the comments! We test the strength and reliability of an old Toyota Land Cruiser, Land Rover Discovery and Mercedes ML by running the cars at full throttle for as long as possible.

Finally, we come to the Land Cruiser. It’s powered by a 4.7-litre V8, and this puts down 235hp and 434Nm. It rolls up with a 4-speed automatic gearbox, and it weighs 2,260kg. videos.title: Rotera med en mening…
videos.channel: Bättre & Enklare golf med Marcus Edblad PGA
videos.summary_source_text:
Rotera med en mening… Vi behöver rotera för att hjälpa våra händer förflytta sig men allt annan rotation är bortkastad energi.

#golf #golftips #golfswing 

0:00 Intro till lär dig rotera med en mening. 

Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du kämpar med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook. Vi behöver rotera för att hjälpa våra händer förflytta sig men allt annan rotation är bortkastad energi.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par.

videos.title: 20.07.26 - HIP-4 Explained: The Future of Prediction Markets on Hyperliquid
videos.summary_source_text:
20.07.26 - HIP-4 Explained: The Future of Prediction Markets on Hyperliquid Welcome to Bonus Episode 3 of the RIFT Before You Quit series on Predictions market.

In this special technical deep dive, we explore HIP-4 (Hyperliquid Improvement Proposal 4) and how it forms the foundation for the next generation of on-chain prediction markets inside the RIFT ecosystem.

Prediction markets are much more than simple yes-or-no betting systems. They require sophisticated infrastructure capable of creating outcome contracts, matching buyers and sellers, managing collateral, recording ownership, resolving real-world events, and distributing payouts transparently. This episode explains how Hyperliquid's HIP-4 framework provides that infrastructure and why it represents a major advancement in decentralized financial markets.

Topics covered include:

- What HIP-4 is and why it was created
- How prediction markets differ from perpetual futures trading
- The importance of fully collateralized outcome contracts
- Why outcome markets require specialised blockchain infrastructure
- Binary markets, range markets and multi-outcome contracts
- How outcome shares are created, traded and settled
- Understanding market probabilities through pricing
- Order books within prediction markets
- Event resolution and transparent settlement mechanisms
- How collateral protects participants
- Why prediction markets cannot rely on simple web interfaces alone
- The role of smart contracts in automated settlement
- Market liquidity and capital efficiency
- Risk management within outcome-based trading
- How prediction market contracts are represented on Hyperliquid
- Why bounded outcomes create predictable maximum risk
- The technical differences between prediction markets and leveraged derivatives
- How traders can enter and exit positions before final settlement
- Why prediction markets are active trading markets rather than traditional wagering systems
- The importance of trusted data sources and objective event resolution
- Infrastructure required to support decentralized prediction markets at scale

The episode also explains why RIFT chose to build its prediction market framework on Hyperliquid's HIP-4 infrastructure rather than developing an entirely separate settlement engine. By leveraging specialised protocol-level functionality, RIFT can focus on delivering an intuitive trading experience while benefiting from transparent on-chain execution, efficient order matching and secure settlement.

You'll also gain an understanding of how prediction markets may eventually integrate with the wider RIFT ecosystem, including Smart Money, automated trading infrastructure, market intelligence tools and future AI-powered trading systems.

Whether you're new to prediction markets or want to understand the technology powering one of RIFT's newest features, this episode provides a comprehensive introduction to the mechanics, architecture and future potential of decentralized outcome markets.

As blockchain technology continues to evolve, prediction markets are expected to become an increasingly important component of decentralized finance, allowing participants to trade on real-world events through transparent, fully collateralized and non-custodial infrastructure.

On today's show, we break down Spain's dramatic 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina in the FIFA World Cup Final, discuss what could be next for the 39-year-old Messi, and examine one of the greatest goalkeeping performances in World Cup history from Emiliano Martínez.
