videos.title: Hindi - 20.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 20.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

videos.title: Bedard Signs New Contract | Which Players Would Avs Freeze If Another Expansion Draft Took Place?
videos.summary_source_text:
Bedard Signs New Contract | Which Players Would Avs Freeze If Another Expansion Draft Took Place? Connor Bedard inks a 5-year, $15 million AAV deal with the Chicago Blackhawks, raising fresh questions about modern NHL contract strategies. Is Bedard leaving money on the table, or betting on himself with flexible term in a rising salary cap era? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson compare Bedard’s blockbuster contract to Leo Carlsson’s, discuss the implications for Chicago’s rebuild, and speculate how future deals—like Cale Makar’s potentially massive extension with the Colorado Avalanche—might reshape the league’s financial landscape.

The conversation shifts to a hypothetical NHL expansion draft: which Colorado Avalanche players would be protected or exposed? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson make their picks, debate keeping veterans like Nazem Kadri or Logan O’Connor, and consider sleeper choices for an expansion roster. Plus, hear a first-hand account of encountering the Stanley Cup up close. How would you freeze the Avalanche roster if the draft happened today?

videos.title: Rotate with a purpose…
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Rotate with a purpose… Everything we do should have a proper purpose and rotation without a good reason is just stupid. Rotation should happen to support how you want to move the club. 

#golfswing #golf #golftips 

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

0:00 Intro to how to rotate with a purpose in an effortless golf swing. 

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 Everything we do should have a proper purpose and rotation without a good reason is just stupid. Rotation should happen to support how you want to move the club. 

#golfswing #golf #golftips

Ryan Koenigsberg, Jake Schwanitz and Scott Procter share their thoughts on Georgia Tech EDGE rusher Brayden Manely calling Julian Lewis “a little kid” and how this is even more motivation for the Buffs heading into the 2026 season. videos.title: 6 Everyday Things That Are Dirtier Than You Think | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
6 Everyday Things That Are Dirtier Than You Think | Dr. Mandell Some of the dirtiest things in your home are the everyday objects and surfaces you touch without giving them a second thought. In this video, you’ll discover six common household germ traps, including what can happen when you flush the toilet with the lid open, why your kitchen sponge may hold more bacteria than you realize, why wet utensils should not go back into the drawer, how a damp bath mat can contribute to foot fungus, why your remote control may be one of the dirtiest objects in your living room, and how the bottoms of your shoes can carry dirt and germs throughout your home. These simple tips can help you improve household hygiene, reduce the spread of bacteria and fungus, and create a cleaner, healthier environment for you and your family.

videos.summary_source_text:
Instead of bread! A PROTEIN breakfast in 10 minutes! ONLY 3 ingredients! Instead of bread! A PROTEIN breakfast in 10 minutes! ONLY 3 ingredients!

1¼ cups (200 g) ricotta.
2/3 cup (150 ml) water. 1 tbsp psyllium husk.
1 tsp baking soda. 2 tbsp brown or black rice. 1/2 tsp salt.
Let the dough rest for 15 mins.
Divide the dough into 3 equal portions.
Optional: sprinkle with sesame seeds on top.
Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (180°C) for about 25 mins.

videos.title: This ring replaces your keyboard?
videos.summary_source_text:
This ring replaces your keyboard? This RING Replaces Your Keyboard??

Meet the OASIS 1, a titanium smart ring designed to change how you interact with your devices.

Instead of typing, you simply whisper your emails, messages, or AI prompts. The ring's noise-isolating microphone captures your voice, and Wispr Flow instantly converts it into text.

To navigate, the top of the ring features a miniature 2D trackpad with haptic feedback, allowing you to scroll, move your cursor, and edit text using just your thumb.

I haven't seen any AI device like this before.

Would you swap your keyboard and mouse for a smart ring? Let me know your thoughts in the comments! 👇

videos.title: She Couldn’t Hit Her Driver Straight…Until We Fixed This Downswing Move
videos.summary_source_text:
She Couldn’t Hit Her Driver Straight…Until We Fixed This Downswing Move ⛳️ Take Your FREE Game Assessment → https://meandmygolf.com/game-plan/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=20.07.26

📱 Download the Swing Coach App (2-Week Free Trial) → https://go.meandmygolf.com/swingcoach/yt/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=20.07.26

Christie was an athletic golfer, but her driver swing was getting steep and working a little over the top. The main reason was her lower body was swaying, which left her upper body leading the downswing and caused the club to cut across the ball.

In this lesson, we worked on stabilising the lower body, improving her position at the top, and then using the lower body to lead the downswing. Once her body sequence improved, the club started to shallow, her path became more neutral, and her drives started flying much straighter.

If your driver swing gets steep, you come over the top, or you struggle to stop cutting across the ball, this lesson will show you how the body can help create a better downswing without forcing the club into position.

videos.title: Grok 5 Explained: The $60 Billion AI Bet Nobody Is Talking About
videos.summary_source_text:
Grok 5 Explained: The $60 Billion AI Bet Nobody Is Talking About Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
What if Grok 5's biggest upgrade isn't more parameters—but better coding data?

Elon Musk's xAI reportedly made a massive $60 billion move by acquiring Cursor AI, and it could completely reshape how Grok 5 competes with GPT-6, Claude, and every other AI coding assistant.

In this video, we break down:

✅ Why Cursor AI matters more than parameter counts
✅ How Grok 5 could become one of the best coding AI models
✅ What this acquisition means for developers
✅ Why everyone is focusing on the wrong metric
✅ The biggest limitations no one is talking about

Rather than chasing another AI hype cycle, we examine what this acquisition actually means for Grok 5's coding capabilities, pricing strategy, transparency, and competition with OpenAI and Anthropic.

03:00 What Will Be Inside Grok 5's Coding Core

06:55 Why Cursor AI—and Why Now?

10:05 Final Verdict: Is Grok 5 Worth the Hype?

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

• Drivers

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.

• Amazing golf courses

• Worldwide golf adventures

videos.title: Your Diet Programs Your Baby
videos.summary_source_text:
Your Diet Programs Your Baby Science shows that what you eat during pregnancy may influence how your baby’s genes behave for the rest of their life. How crazy is that.

Scientists call this epigenetics. Think of it like dimmer switches sitting on top of your DNA. These switches can turn certain genes on or off depending on the environment in the womb.

Nutrients, stress, sleep, and metabolic health all send messages about what kind of world the baby should prepare for.

This is powerful because it shows that parents may have influence long before birth. Small daily choices may help build a stronger foundation for brain development, metabolism, and lifelong health.

This is why I wrote a book all about pregnancy nutrition. It's called 9 Months That Count Forever and contains all the science and easy practical tips I used during my own pregnancy.

videos.title: So what does chapstick have to do with golf???
videos.summary_source_text:
So what does chapstick have to do with golf??? Raise your hand if you have a slice 🙋🏼‍♀️

No, we’re not encouraging any cheating on the links - but plenty of you have asked us to prove (or disprove) the age-old “member guest” hack.

Chapstick, Vaseline and even PAM have been spotted in golfer’s bags as a way to reduce friction off the face...therefore...reducing your spin.

QUICK TEST:
➡️ 10 shots
➡️ 100 mph
➡️ Beez Nuts Balms Coating

If you don’t mind the product being non-conforming (AND YOU’RE NOT COMPETING), $4 chapstick could be the solution over a $600 driver.

Know someone who’s spraying it right...constantly?

SHARE 👉🏽 this with a golfer in need 😂

videos.title: 7 Reasons You Suck at Golf
videos.summary_source_text:
7 Reasons You Suck at Golf Stop wasting shots! In this video, we share 7 simple golf course management strategy tips to instantly drop your handicap and stop making dumb mistakes that ruin your round. Whether you are trying to break 100, break 90, or break 80 in golf, mastering your tee shot strategy, recovery shots, and smart scoring habits is the easiest way to lower golf scores without changing your swing mechanics.

00:00 - The 7 Dumb Mistakes Killing Your Golf Score
00:25 - Tee Shot Strategy: Driver vs Safe Options
01:30 - The Hero Flop Shot vs High-Percentage Chips
02:35 - Wasting Short Putts & Handling Frustration
03:30 - How to Play Recovery Shots (Stop Chipping Sideways!)
04:20 - Smart Strategy for Carrying Water Hazards
05:10 - How to Avoid Sucker Pins & Front/Back Pin Placement
06:10 - Why You Should Ditch the 3-Wood Off the Deck
07:05 - Stop Making These Golf Scoring Errors!

- Why driver isn't always the best club off the tee
- How to stop taking on low-percentage flop shots
- The mistake golfers make after missing birdie putts
- How to recover from trouble without making a bigger mistake
- Why carrying hazards often costs more shots than it saves
- How to identify "sucker pins" and choose smarter targets
- When to leave the 3-wood in the bag

If you're trying to break 100, break 90 or finally break 80, improving your decisions can lower your scores faster than changing your golf swing.

Let me know in the comments: Which of these mistakes costs you the most shots?

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/3SCfAWr

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Here are a few ways to change your environment, reset your dopamine, and regain control:

• Remove junk food from the house
• Distance yourself from unhealthy influences
• Spend more time in nature
• Create more downtime
• Use ashwagandha
• Make unhealthy habits less convenient

Sometimes the simplest solution is to make the habit harder to access.

Eric Cogorno shows exactly why low and slow sends the club too far inside and under plane — and replaces it with two simple mantras any golfer can use: wide and up with the arms and club, centered and turned with the body. Watch how Adam Scott and Nelly Korda start their golf backswing with the club head above the hip while the hands stay below — and how the lead hand and trail hand drills give you the same feeling in minutes. These golf backswing tips apply to any golfer regardless of handicap or ability level. If you leave this video without trying the lead hand only drill, you're leaving a better golf swing takeaway on the table.

• Why low and slow in the golf swing takeaway sends the club too far inside and under plane
• The wide and up mantra: butt of the club outside the trail thigh, club head above the hip

• The centered and turned body mantra that prevents the upper body from drifting away from the target
• The lead hand only drill for training width and centered turn in the golf backswing
• The trail hand only drill for exaggerating width and feeling the correct takeaway position
• How to use the Swing Coach app to check swing plane and spine position during the takeaway
• How getting the golf swing takeaway right makes everything in the downswing easier

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Corales Puntacana | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Corales Puntacana | 2026 Check out all the best shots from the 2026 Corales Puntacana Championship, featuring Ben James, Will Gordon, Gordon Sargent and Stefano Mazzoli, among others.

The PGA TOUR heads to the Dominican Republic this week for the Corales Puntacana Championship, the fourth Additional Event of the 2026 season. Co-sanctioned with the DP World Tour and contested alongside The Open Championship, the tournament makes its first appearance in July after previously occupying an April date, presenting a different test at the scenic Corales Golf Course. Defending champion Garrick Higgo returns looking to become the first two-time winner of the event, while Taylor Pendrith arrives with momentum after his playoff runner-up finish at the ISCO Championship. Joel Dahmen also returns to the site of his lone PGA TOUR victory, hoping to rebound after narrowly missing out on the title a year ago. The field is further strengthened by top-ranked Germans Matti Schmid and Stephan Jaeger, while Aaron Wise continues his return to PGA TOUR competition following an encouraging T3 finish at the ISCO Championship. More than 40 DP World Tour members are also set to tee it up, highlighting the event's international appeal. With 300 FedExCup points on the line and valuable opportunities remaining before the closing stretch of the season, the Corales Puntacana Championship once again promises a compelling week of golf on one of the PGA TOUR's most picturesque venues.

videos.title: Kimi Antonelli On Winning At Spa | Belgian GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Kimi Antonelli On Winning At Spa | Belgian GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast Tom Clarkson is joined by race-winner Kimi Antonelli, F1TV lead presenter Laura Winter and two-time Le Mans podium finisher Alex Brundle to review the Belgian Grand Prix. 

Kimi converted pole to secure his sixth win of the season and move 45 points clear at the top of the World Championship. 12 months on from last year’s Belgian Grand Prix, one of his most challenging weekends of the 2025 season, how has the 19-year-old made such an incredible transformation?  

Kimi joins us in the Mercedes motorhome to reflect on his victory and a close fight with Ferrari’s Charles Leclerc.  

You’ll also hear reaction from George Russell, whose DNF after a collision with Lewis Hamilton on lap 1 leaves him 50 points adrift of Antonelli. What do the guys make of that incident, the comments George has made about his car’s problems and where he goes from here? 

Tom, Laura and Alex also discuss how surprised they were by Ferrari’s pace, Max Verstappen’s third podium of the season, and whether Lando Norris could have won without his grid penalty.

00:00 - 'So mature and measured'
05:23 - Kimi talks through his win
12:24 - Can George find an answer?
19:48 - Why Hungary is 'wide open'
31:09 - Ferrari's Spa surprise
46:28 - Red Bull 'on a knife-edge'
50:43 - Did McLaren miss out?
55:38 - Hadjar for DOTD?

videos.title: Jaecoo 8 Review: PROOF German SUVs are overpriced!
videos.summary_source_text:
Jaecoo 8 Review: PROOF German SUVs are overpriced! This is our in-depth review of the Jaecoo 8 SUV. We’ll run you through the design, the interior, what it’s like to drive, and test its acceleration from 0mph to 60mph.

Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://www.carvertical.com/carwow

This is the all-new Jaecoo 8! 

It’s a competitor to renowned, established family SUVs like the Skoda Kodiaq and Hyundai Santa Fe. The question is, does this all-new Chinese competitor have what it takes to overcome them? Let’s take a closer look… 

Starting with the design, it’s fair to say Jaecoo seems to have taken some inspiration from Audi! The shape and size of the grille, coupled with the headlight design, look to have been lifted from an Audi Q7! The vertical slats within the grille also seem incredibly similar to what recent Mercedes SUVs have to offer. Around the back, there’s a full-length light bar, a roof spoiler, and the brand’s name is embossed across the boot - as seen in almost every new car these days! All in all, it looks like a premium car from whatever angle you look at it. 

Step inside, and it seems Jaecoo has once again taken inspiration from another brand - but this time it’s Mercedes instead of Audi! The positioning of the vents, buttons and drive selector stalks all seem similar to what you’ll find in a Mercedes, even if the quality is a bit lower down the pecking order. There’s a relatively large infotainment screen and digital dial display which serve their purpose well enough, and the cabin is relatively well built. There’s also a good amount of storage throughout. 

The Jaecoo 8 is powered by a 1.5-litre hybrid petrol engine that can deliver 428hp. This powers the front wheels, and it can accelerate to 60mph in just 5.8 seconds. 

The Jaecoo 8 starts from just £45,500, so it’s relatively in line with competitors like the Santa Fe. The thing is, whereas top-of-the-range models of the Santa Fe increase up to around £60,000, there aren’t various different trims to consider with the Jaecoo - all models come with the same features and tech!

So would you be ready to choose this 7-seater over its rivals? Stick with Mat to see if you should!

Mat's Jaecoo 8 choice: https://bit.ly/Mats-Jaecoo-8-Choice

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-2007

Hyundai Santa Fe review: https://bit.ly/Hyundai-Santa-Fe-2007

Skoda Kodiaq review: https://bit.ly/Skoda-Kodiaq-2007

Do forgiving clubs IMPROVE your score? 🤔 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.

videos.title: Why Women in 50s Have Shoulder Problems?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Women in 50s Have Shoulder Problems? Why do so many women get frozen shoulder in their 50s? 

One of the biggest reasons appears to be the hormonal changes that happen during perimenopause and menopause.

When estrogen drops, it sets off a cascade of changes:

• Inflammation tends to increase.
• The shoulder becomes more prone to fibrosis (excess scar tissue), which can limit movement.
• Blood sugar regulation often gets worse, leading to more advanced glycation end products (AGEs), which can increase inflammation and make connective tissue stiffer.

Put all of that together, and it's not surprising that frozen shoulder is so common in women between 45 and 60.

The good news? 

Frozen shoulder is usually temporary.

For most people, it takes 1 to 3 years to resolve, but with patience, smart movement, and a progressive rehab program, there's a very good chance you'll regain most or all of your shoulder motion.

The biggest mistake is assuming it's your new normal.

It usually isn't.

QUESTION: Have you dealt with frozen shoulder yourself? Why do so many women get frozen shoulder in their 50s?

• Blood sugar regulation often gets worse, leading to more advanced glycation end products (AGEs), which can increase inflammation and make connective tissue stiffer.

Put all of that together, and it's not surprising that frozen shoulder is so common in women between 45 and 60.

The good news?

videos.title: CUP DAY IN JERSEY! Carolina Hurricanes' Nic Deslauriers Celebrates with Cup + Neuchev Re-Signed
videos.summary_source_text:
CUP DAY IN JERSEY! Carolina Hurricanes' Nic Deslauriers Celebrates with Cup + Neuchev Re-Signed As we're now 71 days from opening night for the Carolina Hurricanes, what is the most recent news surrounding the team and the NHL as a whole? We have a cup celebration in New Jersey, a player re-signs, and the NHL's contract sticker prices are on the increase. Welcome to a new era of the National Hockey League! On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes,  Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on Nic Deslauriers' day with the cup and how the newest hype man has planted himself as a loved teammate in the locker room. How much did it mean to Deslauriers to get his name on the cup with the Hurricanes, and how he has already been placed in high esteem not only with players, but the franchise overall? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on the re-signing of Viktor Neuchev. What does it mean for the Hurricanes and the Chicago Wolves? What can people expect from Neuchev? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on the NHL as a whole. Between Leo Carlsson getting a massive payday and Connor Bedard's new deal, we are entering a new era of the NHL. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: 474. Rikedomskiftet för ett rikare liv: fyra faser och sex förflyttningar | Med Moa Diseborn
videos.summary_source_text:
474. Rikedomskiftet för ett rikare liv: fyra faser och sex förflyttningar | Med Moa Diseborn Vilka är skiftena på den ekonomiska resan? Vilka är fällorna de flesta hamnar i? Vad händer när mer pengar inte förändrar vardagen i någon större omfattning? Det är frågan bakom Moas modell "The Wealth Shift". Avsnittet blev ärligt talat också mer personligt än jag hade tänkt från början eftersom jag insåg tydligt var jag själv fortfarande sitter fast. 

Moa har kokat ner alla år vi jobbat med de här frågorna till en egen modell med fyra faser (get rich, stay rich, live rich, leave rich) och sex tankeskiften man behöver rör sig genom längs den ekonomiska resan och när pengarna väl finns där.

Det handlar om att gå från ett bristtänk till att våga tänka att det finns tillräckligt, från prestation och nyttovärde till identitet, från att samla på hög till att veta vad man faktiskt vill. Och det viktigaste: de flesta av skiftena går att börja med oavsett var du står ekonomiskt.

Några av ämnena vi tar upp i veckans avsnitt:
• Varför vi nästan aldrig pratar om vad som händer när pengarna väl finns där
• De fyra faserna: get rich, stay rich, live rich och leave rich
• Från brist till tillräckligt, och varför "nästa miljon" aldrig räcker
• Från prestation till identitet: vem du är när du inte längre behöver bevisa något
• Från ackumulering till avsikt, när nästa miljon tappar sin lockelse
• Ensamheten som kan följa med framgång, och vägen till tillhörighet
• Från framgång till bidrag: vad du vill förstärka i världen
• Arv som något du lever redan i dag, inte bara lämnar efter dig

Vi hoppas att du gillar avsnittet,
Jan, Caroline och Moa Diseborn

Innehållsförteckning och kapitel:
====
00:00:00 – Introduktion till avsnittet
00:03:00 – Varför vi aldrig pratar om vad som händer sen
00:04:37 – De fyra faserna: get rich, stay rich, live rich, leave rich

01:04:35 – Nästa steg i morgon: lilla stegets kraft
01:07:57 – Sammanfattning: sårbarhet som generositet och provinspelning

videos.title: The Vitamin That Helps Keep Calcium Out of Your Arteries | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
The Vitamin That Helps Keep Calcium Out of Your Arteries | Dr. Mandell Vitamin K2 has become one of the most talked-about nutrients for supporting healthy aging, strong bones, and cardiovascular health—but what does the science really show? In this video, Dr. Alan Mandell explains how vitamin K2 activates important proteins involved in calcium regulation, helping your body direct calcium to your bones and helping inhibit inappropriate calcium deposits in soft tissues such as your arteries. You'll learn the difference between vitamin K1 and vitamin K2, why vitamin K1 is abundant in leafy green vegetables, and why vitamin K2 is found primarily in fermented foods such as natto, certain aged cheeses, egg yolks, and some animal products. I also explain the two most common supplemental forms of vitamin K2—MK-4 and MK-7—why MK-7 is the form most often chosen for daily supplementation because it remains active in the bloodstream longer, and how to take it for optimal absorption with a meal containing healthy fats. We'll discuss how vitamin D3 and vitamin K2 work together in calcium metabolism, the differences between arterial calcification and cholesterol plaque, who may benefit from increasing K2-rich foods, and when supplementation may be appropriate. Most importantly, you'll learn why no vitamin can replace controlling blood pressure, cholesterol, blood sugar, inflammation, exercise, and a healthy diet when it comes to protecting your heart and blood vessels. I also cover important safety precautions, including why anyone taking warfarin (Coumadin) or other medications should speak with their healthcare professional before starting vitamin K2 supplements. If you've ever wondered whether vitamin K2 can help support healthier arteries and stronger bones, this evidence-based video will help separate scientific facts from myths so you can make informed decisions about your health.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: Why poor weight shift kills iron compression #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Why poor weight shift kills iron compression #golfswing #golftips Many golfers make what feels like a good swing but still struggle to compress their irons. When pressure stays on the back foot and doesn’t move into the lead side, the low point shifts too far back, leading to thin strikes and weak ball flight. Improving weight shift helps you strike the ball first and produce more powerful, consistent contact.
If iron strike or compression is holding your game back, head to the link in our bio. Our coaching plans give you clear guidance on weight shift, low point, and impact so you can hit your irons with confidence.

videos.title: How to Get "Bigger Arms" Fast (22 DAYS | NO B.S)
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Get "Bigger Arms" Fast (22 DAYS | NO B.S) Build Bigger Arms Here - https://athleanx.com
Subscribe to this channel here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

Want to know how to get bigger arms in just 22 days? In this ATHLEAN-X arm workout, Jeff Cavaliere lays out a step-by-step plan to help you build bigger biceps, bigger triceps, and more noticeable arm size without adding a full arm day to your weekly split.

Building big arms takes more than doing endless curls and triceps extensions. You need the right exercises, enough training frequency, progressive overload, and quality repetitions that place tension on the muscles you are trying to grow. This 22-day bigger arms workout trains the biceps and triceps three times per week with short sessions that can be added to the end of your current workouts.

If you are following a total-body routine, push pull legs split, upper lower split, or bro split, keep your compound exercises. Simply remove your existing direct biceps and triceps work and replace it with the exercises shown here.

Start by measuring your flexed arm around the highest point of the biceps and thickest portion of the triceps. Record it on day one, then repeat the measurement under the same conditions on day 22.

The first biceps exercise is the incline dumbbell waiter curl. Keeping the top of the dumbbell level limits unwanted shoulder involvement and keeps tension on the biceps near the top of the rep. Perform four sets of nine reps. In week two, add three pulses to every rep. In week three, increase that to six pulses. These contractions improve the mind-muscle connection and make the shortened position more demanding.

The first triceps exercise is the bench dip, performed for four sets to technical failure. Turn the hands slightly outward on the bench to encourage a safer shoulder position, lower under control, and finish every rep by fully extending the elbows. Add three pulses at lockout during week two and six pulses during week three to increase the time under tension.

To build more width through the upper arm, the next biceps exercise targets the brachialis. The alternating cross-body hammer curl uses a neutral grip and brings the dumbbell across the body, forcing the brachialis to work harder. Begin with four sets of 12 reps per arm. In week two, add five pounds and use rest-pause training to complete all 12 reps. In week three, add another five pounds and finish the target reps with good form.

For the medial head of the triceps, choose either the triceps pushdown or the thumbs-up dumbbell bench press. Both emphasize complete elbow extension, which is often missing from poorly performed triceps workouts. On pushdowns, allow the wrists to extend naturally so the forearms do not dominate the exercise. On the dumbbell press, keep the elbows close and drive the thumbs upward as you reach full lockout. Add five pounds in week two and another five pounds in week three, using rest-pause as needed to reach 12 quality reps.

The final biceps and triceps sessions focus on eccentric overload, an effective way to increase mechanical tension and stimulate muscle growth. For biceps, use the cheat curl. Apply only enough momentum to bring the weight to the top, then make the biceps control the lowering phase for three seconds. The next week, extend the eccentric to four seconds. During the final week, begin with five-second negatives, then reduce to four seconds and three seconds as fatigue builds.

For triceps, perform the PJR pullover. Use the lats to help return the dumbbell to the starting position, but make the long head of the triceps control the slow lowering and stretch behind the head. The eccentric progresses from three seconds to four seconds, then to a final five-, four-, and three-second sequence during the last week.

This big arms workout combines frequency, focused volume, progressive overload, rest-pause training, and controlled eccentrics. Instead of training biceps and triceps once per week, the work is spread across multiple sessions with recovery between them, giving you more opportunities to stimulate stubborn arm muscles.

Follow the 22-day schedule, keep your form strict, and do not add excessive junk volume. The goal is to make every curl, press, dip, pushdown, and pullover count. On day 22, measure your arms again and compare the result with your starting number. Share your arm gains in the comments and challenge a training partner to complete the full arm workout with you.

videos.title: Good at Practice, Bad in Games? This Is Why.
videos.summary_source_text:
Good at Practice, Bad in Games? This Is Why. Good at Practice, Bad in Games? This Is Why.

Some players look unstoppable at practice and then disappear the moment the game starts. Others play on pure instinct in games but never actually get better week to week. Most people blame effort or talent, but there's one hidden reason this keeps happening, and it has nothing to do with how hard you work.

Jason breaks down the single mindset error that quietly wrecks both your development and your game-day performance, and the simple rule for knowing which mode you should be in and when. Watch the full breakdown to see how to switch it on at the right time so you actually grow in practice and lock in when it counts.

videos.title: If You Can't Do This, You're Not Fit (Even If You Look It)
videos.summary_source_text:
If You Can't Do This, You're Not Fit (Even If You Look It) Jeff Cavaliere and Dr. Andrew Huberman discuss the 'old man test' and other functional assessments for longevity, emphasizing how simple daily movements that challenge balance, pelvic control, and glute medius strength can reveal physical weaknesses and be trained to support long-term musculoskeletal health.

Subscribe to RESPIRE for more science-based health tips: 
   / @respireofficial  
re·spire (verb) – (1) to breathe (2) to recover hope, courage, or strength

videos.title: Great Drill to Stop Leading with Your Arms #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Great Drill to Stop Leading with Your Arms #golfswing #golftips If you’re struggling to compress your irons or make solid contact, this drill will change your feel instantly. Too many golfers get the classic chicken wing in their arms, leading with that and neglecting the body rotation, causing thin and inconsistent strikes. Try the push ball drill — keeping the arms straight and rotating your body through.
This teaches proper rotation, better extension, and that pure, ball-then-turf compression you see from great ball strikers.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. If you’re struggling to compress your irons or make solid contact, this drill will change your feel instantly. This teaches proper rotation, better extension, and that pure, ball-then-turf compression you see from great ball strikers.

videos.title: Try The Happy Gilmore Technique #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Try The Happy Gilmore Technique #golfswing #golftips If you struggle with posture loss or inconsistent contact, this drill will help you feel the right movement instantly. By splitting your hands and gripping the club like a hockey stick, you can train proper posture, rotation, and shaft movement without worrying about hitting a ball. Striking an impact bag exaggerates the correct chest rotation and helps you learn how real compression should feel. Build this into your practice and your ball-striking will improve fast.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. If you struggle with posture loss or inconsistent contact, this drill will help you feel the right movement instantly. videos.summary_source_text:
The #1 Most Dangerous Food For Your Body The most dangerous food in your kitchen may not be what you think. Highly processed seed oils are refined with heat, chemicals, and solvents, making them especially prone to oxidation before they ever reach your plate.

🌟Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/3T7SZSc

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Common seed oils include:
• Canola oil
• Corn oil
• Soybean oil
• Cottonseed oil
• Sunflower oil
• Safflower oil

These oils are especially sensitive to heat, light, and oxygen. Repeated heating, such as in restaurant fryers, can increase oxidation and the formation of additional breakdown byproducts. Choosing less processed fats and avoiding repeatedly heated seed oils can help reduce your exposure to these oxidized compounds.

Recept:
Thai Sticky Rice med Mango (Khao Niew Mamuang)

Thai Sticky Rice med Mango är en magisk kombination av krämigt kokosris, med en söt och saftig mango. Det är en klassisk thailändsk efterrätt, enkel men otroligt god! Traditionellt så ska man blötlägga riset i flera timmar eller gärna över natten och även ånga det. Men i detta receptet har jag skyndat på processen en aning och gjort det lite lättare att få till hemma i köket.
Vill du byta ut det klibbiga riset? Så funkar sushiris med.
60 min
4p
Ingredienser:
Sticky Rice:
3 dl klibbigt ris (glutinous rice) ( eller sushi ris)
Vatten för blötläggning
400 ml kokosmjölk
4dl vatten
3 msk strösocker
1 nypa salt 

Kokosgrädde till topping:
2dl kokosgrädde
2 msk socker
½ tsk majsstärkelse + 1 msk vatten (för att tjockna såsen)

Till servering:
2 mogna mango, skivade
1 msk rostadekokosflingor
Färsk mynta (valfritt)
Gör så här:
Skölj riset i kallt vatten tills vattnet är klart. Blötlägg i minst 10min men gärna över natten.
Sila riset och lägg i en kastrull, gå i med kokosmjölken, vatten, socker och salt och koka upp riset. Sänk sedan till medelvärme och rör om konstant eller med jämna mellanrum så att riset inte bränner fast, i ca 10min. Tillsätt extra vatten om det skulle behövas. Sätt lock på och sänk värmen till låg. Låt stå i ca 20min. 


Kokosgrädde till topping: Värm 2dl kokosgrädde med socker. Blanda majsstärkelse med vatten och rör ner för att tjockna såsen lätt. Låt svalna.
Blanda sedan såsen med riset så att riset blir krämigt! 
Lägg upp det riset på en tallrik, toppa med färsk mango Strö över rostade kokosflingor och ev ett myntablad.

videos.title: Sveriges billigaste burgare utmanar den dyraste
videos.summary_source_text:
Sveriges billigaste burgare utmanar den dyraste 📖 Köp vår bok: https://www.adlibris.com/sv/bok/burgare-sa-gor-du-varldens-basta-burgare-hemma-9789127191211

Den ena kostar under hundralappen och har utsetts till Årets burgarkedja tre gånger. Den andra serveras på Restaurang AG, görs på 200 dagar hängmörat Rubia Gallega-kött och kostar nästan fyra gånger så mycket.

Men är den verkligen fyra gånger bättre?

I det här avsnittet ställer vi Sveriges billigaste hantverksburgare mot Sveriges dyraste i ett smaktest där pris, råvaror, utförande och smak får avgöra. Är Mister York den bästa burgaren för pengarna, eller lever AG upp till den höga prislappen? Nu tar vi reda på om lyx verkligen smakar bättre.

videos.title: Can You Re-Grow Knee Cartilage? (maybe)
videos.summary_source_text:
Can You Re-Grow Knee Cartilage? (maybe) Can you regrow knee cartilage? 

Maybe.

In November 2025, Stanford researchers published a study in Science showing that blocking an aging related enzyme called 15-PGDH triggered the regeneration of articular (hyaline) cartilage in mice. The treatment also prevented osteoarthritis from developing after knee injuries in mice, and human cartilage samples responded by producing new cartilage in the lab. 

That's a huge breakthrough.

But here's the reality:

We still don't know if the same results will happen in people. Human clinical trials are needed, and safety is the biggest unanswered question. Any therapy that changes tissue regeneration has to be carefully studied for unintended effects before it becomes widely available.

That said, this feels like one of those moments that could change the future of joint health.

GLP-1 drugs transformed obesity treatment almost overnight. It's possible that, over the next decade, we'll see a similar breakthrough for cartilage repair.

For anyone living with knee arthritis, that's a reason for cautious optimism. Can you regrow knee cartilage?

videos.title: The McDavid Breakaway Formula
videos.summary_source_text:
The McDavid Breakaway Formula The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body’s own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness.
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

Choosing higher-quality sources can improve the balance of fats you're eating, while still providing the benefits of real, minimally processed food.

Expensive creams and serums often overlook what’s happening beneath the surface. Facial aging involves changes in the fascia (connective tissue), the muscles, and even the underlying bone structure.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4vB8b7M

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

• Supporting the fascia beneath the skin.
• Improving facial support beyond the surface of the skin.
• Why traditional stretching isn’t recommended.
• Techniques that may help improve facial definition and reduce the appearance of sagging over time.

videos.title: This Mobility Test Explains Your Swing  #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
This Mobility Test Explains Your Swing  #golfswing #golftips This simple 90/90 mobility test gives you a clear look at how your right arm should function in the golf swing. From a 5-iron posture, lift and rotate the arm until it matches your spine angle—if you can’t get there, your backswing is likely compensating by standing up or losing posture. That compensation often leads to poor ball striking and inconsistent contact. Your swing should reflect what your body can actually do, even if that means a shorter backswing.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. This simple 90/90 mobility test gives you a clear look at how your right arm should function in the golf swing. videos.title: Stop Wasting Money on This Magnesium!
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Wasting Money on This Magnesium! Not all magnesium supplements are created equal. The type of magnesium you take can make a huge difference in absorption and results.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4uTK4AO

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Common forms of magnesium
• Magnesium oxide: ~4% absorption, high laxative effect
• Magnesium sulfate: ~10% absorption
• Magnesium orotate: ~15% absorption, supports cellular energy and DNA
• Magnesium taurate: ~20% absorption, supports the heart, blood pressure, and nervous system
• Magnesium lactate: ~25% absorption, gentler on digestion, supports energy and cardiovascular health
• Magnesium citrate: ~30% absorption, commonly used for kidney stones, detoxification, and blood pressure support
• Magnesium malate: ~40% absorption, supports energy, muscles, chronic fatigue, and fibromyalgia
• Magnesium threonate: ~75% absorption
• Magnesium glycinate: up to ~80% absorption

Magnesium glycinate is often preferred because it’s highly absorbable, gentle on the digestive system, and commonly used for:
• Sleep
• Relaxation
• Stress
• Muscle spasms
• Migraines

One of magnesium’s most important jobs is helping vitamin D function properly in the body.

#magnesium #magnesiumglycinate #magnesiumdeficiency #sleep #stress #musclespasms #migraine #vitamind #supplements #healthtips

videos.title: What's In Sahith Theegala's Golf Bag? | The Open Special
videos.summary_source_text:
What's In Sahith Theegala's Golf Bag? | The Open Special We take a look inside Sahith Theegala’s golf bag to see the clubs, specs and setup he trusts for The Open. From driver to putter, Sahith talks us through his full WITB and the thinking behind each club.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Who Is Closest to Superintelligence in 2026? OpenAI vs Anthropic vs Google vs xAI
videos.summary_source_text:
Who Is Closest to Superintelligence in 2026? OpenAI vs Anthropic vs Google vs xAI Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Who is actually closest to superintelligence in 2026?

OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and xAI are all racing toward the future of artificial intelligence — but they are not racing toward the same goal.

In this deep dive, we analyze the real AI race using official documents, model releases, benchmarks, pricing, and public statements to answer one question:

Who is actually closest to superintelligence?

We compare:

00:01:09 The only AI lab with an actual superintelligence blueprint
00:03:34 Anthropic: the capability leader nobody can fully use
00:05:18 OpenAI vs Google: racing toward a moving target
00:06:43 xAI Grok: competing on price instead of maximum capability
00:07:45 Microsoft’s unexpected AI strategy shift
00:09:14 Why nobody has created a real superintelligence benchmark
00:10:17 The limitations behind the AI race headlines
00:11:24 The final verdict

The biggest question in AI right now is not simply "who has the smartest model?"

The real question is: who is defining what superintelligence actually means?

This video explores the future of AI, AGI, frontier models, AI safety, OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, xAI Grok, and the race to build the next generation of artificial intelligence.

Subscribe for deep analysis on AI breakthroughs, frontier models, and the future of technology.

The Ball Striking Tip Every Golfer Needs #shorts #golfswing #golf #ericcogorno #shorts 

In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno shows high handicap golfers how hip and leg motion in the backswing creates a flowing transition and better ball striking.

🏌️ WORK WITH ERIC COGORNO IN PERSON!

The BEST Golf Swing Training App Ever Created

Get Custom Golf Instruction from Eric Cogorno
👉 Learn More: https://www.cogornogolf.com

📚 STEP-BY-STEP TRAINING PROGRAMS
Eric's Complete Golf Improvement System
👉 Start Here: https://ericcogornogolf.podia.com

⭐ OUR AWESOME PARTNERS AND DISCOUNT CODES ⭐
🏌️ Takomo Golf Clubs - https://takomogolf.com
👕 Bobby Jones Apparel: https://bobbyjones.com/COGORNO

⛳ theHanger Training Aid - https://www.hangergolf.com/?sca_ref=944104.HqM9GYcX2I (Code: COGORNO for 15% OFF)

If you treat your backswing and downswing as two separate events, this golf instruction lesson will change how you think about your golf swing. Eric Cogorno and Steve Sieracki break down the ground-up sequence — leg flex, trail hip to the midline, hips flowing forward — that separates the best ball strikers from high handicap golfers. Watch how PGA Tour Pro Tommy Fleetwood uses this exact hip and leg motion to create one of the most coordinated, flowing transitions in professional golf — and how these high handicap golf tips give any golfer the same checkpoints to work from. Getting this sequence right gives you better contact, more power, and a more inside-out swing path. If you leave this video without trying the belt buckle forward drill, you're leaving consistency and distance on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• Why high handicap golfers treat the backswing and downswing as two separate events — and how to fix it
• How foot flare and leg flex control hip turn and tilt during the backswing
• How much trail leg flex to lose during the backswing and why it matters for golf instruction
• The trail hip to midline checkpoint: where your trail hip should be at the top of the swing
• How PGA Tour Pro Tommy Fleetwood uses hip and leg motion to create a flowing backswing to downswing transition
• Why the hips start moving toward the target before the backswing is finished
• The belt buckle forward drill for feeling correct hip motion through the ball
• How correct hip and leg motion creates an inside-out swing path for less pulls and fades

videos.title: If Your Struggle To Strike Your Irons, Just Do This Before Every Shot
videos.summary_source_text:
If Your Struggle To Strike Your Irons, Just Do This Before Every Shot If you struggle to strike your irons consistently, hitting the ground before the golf ball is usually at the heart of the problem. One shot comes out fat, the next is thin, and occasionally you top it along the ground.

In this golf lesson, Danny Maude shows you the simple way to create ball-first contact, control the low point of your golf swing and strike your irons more consistently — without rebuilding your entire swing or spending hours on the range.

FREE IRON-STRIKING PRACTICE PLAN

Follow the lesson step by step with Danny’s free practice plan:

https://www.dannymaude.com/simple-way-to-strike-your-irons-consistently

In this video, you’ll discover:

◼ The only contact rule you need to strike your irons consistently
◼ Why fat, thin and topped iron shots often come from the same mistake
◼ How your chest position and weight influence where the club touches the ground
◼ Why keeping your weight forward isn’t always enough to guarantee ball-first contact
◼ How a simple stone-skimming action helps prevent an early release
◼ The two checkpoints that make consistent iron striking much easier
◼ Where to position the golf ball for cleaner, more reliable contact

The goal is simple: strike the golf ball first, then allow the club to touch the ground beyond it.

But to do that consistently, you need to control two things: where your body is positioned relative to the ball and how your trail arm releases the golf club.

Danny explains how keeping your chest and weight forward helps move the bottom of your swing arc beyond the golf ball. He then shows you why many golfers still hit fat and thin shots because their trail arm straightens too early.

Using the feeling of skimming a stone across a pond, you’ll learn how to release the golf club around your body rather than throwing it directly down towards the ground.

This simple combination can help you:

◼ Stop hitting the ground before the golf ball
◼ Reduce fat and thin iron shots
◼ Create more consistent ball-first contact
◼ Improve your low-point control
◼ Strike wedges, mid-irons and long irons more reliably

07:53 Your Iron-Striking Summary
08:33 Does This Work With Fairway Woods?

Swing Coach analyses your swing and shows you what is happening, what needs improving and what to work on next.

Swing Coach is currently available for Apple users.

videos.title: Osteoporosis Isn't a Calcium Deficiency (Doctors Get This Wrong)
videos.summary_source_text:
Osteoporosis Isn't a Calcium Deficiency (Doctors Get This Wrong) Can calcium make osteoporosis worse? If you’re taking a calcium supplement for osteoporosis, it may not be supporting bone strength and bone density the way you think. Discover how to support bone health the right way, and why calcium supplements alone may not be enough. 

0:00 Calcium and osteoporosis
0:45 What causes osteoporosis?
1:04 Magnesium for bones 
1:30 Parathyroid hormone and osteoporosis
2:31 Bone density vs. bone strength
3:38 Magnesium deficiency
4:33 Common osteoporosis treatment 
5:49 How to reverse osteoporosis
6:40 Vitamin D, osteoporosis, and osteomalacia
7:47 Vitamin K2 
8:36 Bone health tips
9:30 Should I take calcium for osteoporosis?

🌟 Download Dr. Berg's Free Daily Health Routine: https://drbrg.co/3SYBeVk

Watch these next to learn more about the nutrients that support healthy bones:
▶️ https://youtu.be/89jJ1JlNZlg
▶️ https://youtu.be/MwZ63SFmkPE
▶️ https://youtu.be/Sn7T46fCjLE

Learn more about osteoporosis and osteopenia in this article: https://drbrg.co/4wPMFgA

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Osteoporosis is not simply a calcium deficiency. Bones store calcium, phosphorus, and more than half of the body’s magnesium. Magnesium helps regulate calcium, supports vitamin D function, and plays an important role in bone health.

One of the most common factors associated with osteoporosis is elevated parathyroid hormone related to low vitamin D. Low magnesium can also affect parathyroid hormone. Some people have normal calcium levels but still develop severe osteoporosis, showing that bone density and bone strength are not always the same.

Magnesium deficiency is difficult to detect with a standard blood test and may go unnoticed until it becomes more severe. Chronic stress and certain medications, including prednisone and proton pump inhibitors (PPIs), may also affect bone health.

If you have osteoporosis, getting enough magnesium is important. Focus on magnesium-rich foods, magnesium glycinate, vitamin D, and vitamin K2 to help support healthy bones and maintain bone density.

Discover how to support bone health the right way, and why calcium supplements alone may not be enough.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Osteoporosis is not simply a calcium deficiency. Bones store calcium, phosphorus, and more than half of the body’s magnesium. Magnesium helps regulate calcium, supports vitamin D function, and plays an important role in bone health.

One of the most common factors associated with osteoporosis is elevated parathyroid hormone related to low vitamin D. Low magnesium can also affect parathyroid hormone. Some people have normal calcium levels but still develop severe osteoporosis, showing that bone density and bone strength are not always the same.

Magnesium deficiency is difficult to detect with a standard blood test and may go unnoticed until it becomes more severe. Chronic stress and certain medications, including prednisone and proton pump inhibitors (PPIs), may also affect bone health.

If you have osteoporosis, getting enough magnesium is important. Focus on magnesium-rich foods, magnesium glycinate, vitamin D, and vitamin K2 to help support healthy bones and maintain bone density.

videos.title: Best-Sounding Drag Race EVER? Porsche Carrera GT v Lexus LFA
videos.summary_source_text:
Best-Sounding Drag Race EVER? Porsche Carrera GT v Lexus LFA We’re drag racing the Porsche Carrera GT and Lexus LFA over the quarter-mile and half-mile. We’ll also be comparing the two in a brake test. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-1807

It’s time for the ultimate Germany vs Japan showdown! 

We’ve got our hands on the exceptional Porsche Carrera GT, and it’s facing off against one of the most famous Japanese exports of all-time - the Lexus LFA! 

The question is, which of these legendary V10s will come out on top? Let’s check out the stats… 

Starting with the Porsche, it’s powered by a 5.7-litre naturally-aspirated V1- that delivers 612hp and 590Nm. This power is sent to the rear wheels only via a 6-speed manual gearbox, and the car weighs in at 1,380kg. When it was first released, it cost around £330,000, but as of today, it’s valued at about £2,000,000! 

Next up, we have the Lexus LFA. It’s powered by a 4.8-litre naturally-aspirated V10, and this creates 560hp and 480Nm. Power is sent to the rear wheels only via a 6-speed auto gearbox, and the car tips the scales at £340,000. As new, it cost around £340,000, but as of today, it’s valued at around £750,000. 

So how will this play out - will the auto gearbox of the LFA power it to victory? Or will the extra power in the Porsche propel it to the finish line first? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE!

videos.title: 17.07.26 - Ep.14 - The AI Capital Era: How Intelligent Systems Will Transform Wealth Forever
videos.summary_source_text:
17.07.26 - Ep.14 - The AI Capital Era: How Intelligent Systems Will Transform Wealth Forever Artificial Intelligence is no longer a concept of the future—it is already reshaping the global financial system. In Episode 14 of RIFT Before You Quit, we explore how AI is transforming modern finance from the inside out, not as a chatbot or trading gimmick, but as the infrastructure behind institutional capital management.

Today's largest financial institutions already rely on AI-powered systems for portfolio allocation, risk management, liquidity monitoring, execution, and market analysis. This episode examines why the next generation of investors may no longer compete through faster reactions or more screen time, but through access to smarter, more adaptive financial systems.

Inside this episode we explore:

✅ Why AI has already become financial infrastructure
✅ How institutions use intelligent systems to manage trillions of dollars
✅ BlackRock's Aladdin platform and why it's changing institutional investing
✅ The evolution from manual trading to machine-assisted participation
✅ Why psychology remains the biggest obstacle for most retail traders
✅ How AI can reduce emotional decision-making in volatile markets
✅ Autonomous portfolio allocation and intelligent capital management
✅ Predictive execution systems and how they prepare before opportunities appear
✅ Why portfolio rebalancing is critical for long-term success
✅ How intelligent capital routing works across crypto, stocks, commodities and cash
✅ The future of AI-powered financial ecosystems
✅ Why adaptive infrastructure is replacing traditional trading methods

The episode also explains the shift from watching charts all day to building structured participation frameworks where intelligent systems assist with monitoring, execution and risk management. Rather than replacing traders, AI is becoming a tool that helps remove repetitive tasks and emotional decision-making, allowing participants to focus on strategy instead of constant market surveillance.

We also discuss how modern institutional systems continuously analyse:

• Market liquidity
• Portfolio exposure
• Volatility
• Correlation shifts
• Risk concentration
• Capital allocation opportunities

These capabilities are gradually becoming available to everyday participants as financial technology continues to evolve.

Throughout the presentation, the discussion connects these industry trends with the long-term vision behind the RIFT ecosystem. Built on Hyperliquid's non-custodial infrastructure, RIFT aims to provide an environment where users can participate across crypto, futures, stocks and commodities through both manual and automated trading tools while maintaining control of their own assets.

The episode outlines how the platform's different participation systems—including AlphaFlow, SmartFlow, MicroFlow, Smart Money, Swipe Trading and future AI-assisted infrastructure—fit into the broader evolution of intelligent financial ecosystems.

Key topics include:

• AI-assisted investing
• Institutional portfolio management
• Risk management systems
• Automated execution
• Predictive market infrastructure
• Machine-assisted trading
• Portfolio balancing
• Capital allocation strategies
• Intelligent financial ecosystems
• The future of wealth participation

As financial markets become increasingly data-driven, the advantage may no longer belong to those with the fastest reactions, but to those with access to the strongest infrastructure.

The AI Capital Era has already begun.

Disclaimer: This video is intended for educational purposes only and should not be considered financial or investment advice. Trading and investing involve risk, and past performance does not guarantee future results. Always carry out your own research before making financial decisions. videos.title: Hindi - 17.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 17.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

videos.summary_source_text:
Is This the Biggest AI Release of 2026? (China’s New DeepSeek Moment) China’s Moonshot AI just released Kimi K3, the world’s largest open-weight AI model. It rivals leading American systems, dominates major coding tests, and arrives as Xi Jinping pushes China to reshape the global AI order.

Get the free 7-agent guide here: https://go.aiskool.io/7agents

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com

📌 What You’ll See:
0:00 — Intro
0:11 — Moonshot introduces Kimi K3, a frontier AI model built for agentic coding, complex projects and long-running knowledge work
SOURCE: https://www.kimi.com/en
0:40 — Reuters examines how the 2.8-trillion-parameter Kimi K3 is closing in on America’s most powerful closed AI models
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/world/china/chinas-moonshot-unveils-worlds-largest-open-ai-model-closing-us-rivals-2026-07-17/
2:13 — Arena’s live leaderboard shows Kimi K3 competing at the top of real-world frontend and web development evaluations
SOURCE: https://arena.ai/leaderboard/code/webdev
11:39 — Reuters explains how Xi Jinping is positioning China as the leader of a new global AI order built around open technology
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/chinas-xi-promotes-chinas-commitment-ai-access-speech-shanghai-conference-2026-07-17/

🚨 Why It Matters
Kimi K3 suggests China may no longer be months behind America in advanced AI. Its scale, open weights, lower pricing and coding performance could pressure OpenAI and Anthropic while strengthening China’s attempt to reshape who controls global AI.

videos.title: Why Do The Best Players’ Blades Chatter?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Do The Best Players’ Blades Chatter? The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

OpenAI reportedly scrapped its original GPT-6 foundation model—internally known as Spud—despite reports suggesting it contained nearly 4 trillion parameters.

In this video, we break down the latest GPT-6 reports, explain why OpenAI may have abandoned a massive training run, and examine how Anthropic's rumored Claude Fable 5.1 could be reshaping the AI race.

Unlike sensational headlines, this video separates confirmed information from leaks, rumors, and speculation to explain what may actually be happening behind the scenes.

videos.title: Kimi K3: Don't Believe the Hype (I Paid to Test)
videos.summary_source_text:
Kimi K3: Don't Believe the Hype (I Paid to Test) Join us at 🎉 https://mrc.fm/cmc

Kimi K3 is sitting at number one on the arena leaderboard above Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 and the internet is calling it the end of American AI. So is it real, or is it all hype and propaganda? I paid Moonshot $19 and ran four identical head to head builds against Claude Fable 5: a playable voxel game, a motion graphics launch video coded from a single prompt, a neon synth pad and a full brand website it had to research itself. Kimi K3 won one of those tests and what happened in the other three is exactly why my money is staying with Claude for now. Watch this before you switch.

videos.title: Advanced Liver Problems Rarely Have Any Symptoms
videos.summary_source_text:
Advanced Liver Problems Rarely Have Any Symptoms Advanced liver problems rarely cause any pain or obvious symptoms, no yellowing skin, no warning signs. Why? Because your liver itself has no nerve endings. But the sac surrounding it does, which is why liver swelling can refer pain to your right shoulder or upper trap.  

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/3StFMm8

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

The good news? A fatty or inflamed liver can be reversed. The key is catching it early, and you often can't rely on symptoms or even standard liver enzyme tests to do that. One simple visual clue: if you're carrying extra belly fat, it may be fat spilling over from an already-overloaded liver. An ultrasound is the most reliable way to check.

#liver #liverproblems #liverdisease #liverhealth #healthtips #health #fattyliver

videos.title: Lower Your Blood Pressure in Minutes with 2 Simple Techniques
videos.summary_source_text:
Lower Your Blood Pressure in Minutes with 2 Simple Techniques You can help lower your blood pressure in just a few minutes using two simple techniques. Slow breathing and brief sun exposure may help support healthy blood pressure naturally.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4vBzDlT

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Try these two approaches:
• Breathe in through your nose for 4 seconds, then exhale slowly through your nose for 6 seconds. Repeat for several minutes.
• Spend about 10 minutes in the sun to expose your skin to natural sunlight, which may help increase nitric oxide and support healthy blood vessel function.

Tracking your blood pressure before and after these techniques can help you see how your body responds.
